name: PocketBase auth action lifecycle
pocketBaseNote: PocketBase auth action endpoints are intentionally quiet for unknown identities and use single-use tokens for verification, password reset, and email change flows.

cases.name: missing verification email requires validation data
cases.pocketBaseNote: PocketBase returns field-level validation data when a required auth action form field is missing.

cases.name: unknown verification email remains silent
cases.pocketBaseNote: PocketBase-style auth action requests do not reveal whether an email address exists.

cases.name: verification token marks record verified
cases.pocketBaseNote: A valid verification token is single-purpose and marks the auth record as verified.

cases.name: reused verification token is rejected
cases.pocketBaseNote: Auth action tokens are single-use and cannot be replayed after successful confirmation.

cases.name: password reset mismatch returns field validation
cases.pocketBaseNote: Password reset confirmation validates password and passwordConfirm before consuming the token.

cases.name: password reset token updates password
cases.pocketBaseNote: A valid password reset token updates the stored password and consumes the token.

cases.name: password reset invalidates previous auth token
cases.pocketBaseNote: After a password reset, existing auth tokens for that record should no longer refresh.

cases.name: old password is denied after reset
cases.pocketBaseNote: The previous password stops authenticating after a successful reset.

cases.name: new password authenticates after reset
cases.pocketBaseNote: The replacement password authenticates through the normal password auth route.
